title: This Championship season has been utterly ridiculo...
summary: England's Championship, already known as the world's most competitive second-tier league, has proven particularly memorable this season with exceptional competitiveness and drama.

zh.summary: 英冠作为世界竞争最激烈的第二级联赛，本赛季格外令人难忘，展现了卓越的竞争力和戏剧性。

title: Clásico kits: Barcelona, Real Madrid's all-time be...

title: Total recall: PSG backs up best team in world talk...
summary: Paris Saint-Germain coach Luis Enrique claimed his team is the best in the world after they defeated Bayern Munich 6-5 on aggregate to reach the Champions League final against Arsenal.

title: Kompany points to Bayern progress after UCL exit

content: Bayern Munich coach Vincent Kompany attempted to focus on the positives of Bayern Munich's Champions League campaign after they bowed out in the semifinals to Paris Saint-Germain on Wednesday.

title: Mexico: Liga MX players late to camp out of WC
summary: Mexico's national soccer federation threatened to exclude Liga MX-based players from World Cup roster consideration if they are late to a mandatory training camp.
content: Mexico has threatened to leave Liga MX-based players out of contention for the World Cup roster if they are late to a training camp that begins on Wednesday night.

title: Man City promise bright 'future' after sealing WSL...

title: Sources: Key players must exit for Barça rebuild
summary: Barcelona must sell key players including Ferran Torres, Jules Koundé, or Raphinha to generate funds for the new signings requested by coach Hansi Flick.
content: Barcelona must raise money through the departure of important players such as Ferran Torres, Jules Koundé or even Raphinha to be able to re-invest in the new signings coach Hansi Flick has requested this summer, various sources have told ESPN.

content: FIFA president Gianni Infantino has again defended high ticket prices for this summer's World Cup and brushed off tickets being resold for the final at a cost of more than $2 million.

title: FIFA extends Prestianni ban to World Cup matches
summary: FIFA extended Gianluca Prestianni's global ban to cover two World Cup matches in the United States if the Benfica winger is selected for Argentina.
content: FIFA confirmed a global ban Wednesday for Gianluca Prestianni that will rule the Benfica winger out of two FIFA World Cup games in the United States if he is selected in Argentina's squad.

content: PSG handled Bayern Munich with ease in Wednesday's semifinal second leg, and now have a shot at a second straight UEFA Champions League. Can Arsenal realistically stop them?
